name: Estadio Azteca
description: Iconic stadium hosting its third World Cup. The Azteca has witnessed legendary moments including Maradona's 'Hand of God' goal and his 'Goal of the Century' in 1986.

name: MetLife Stadium
description: Home of both the New York Giants and Jets, MetLife will host the grand finale — the 2026 World Cup Final — in the heart of the New York metropolitan area.

name: SoFi Stadium
description: State-of-the-art stadium near Los Angeles with a translucent roof and a massive double-sided video board. One of the most technologically advanced venues in the world.

name: AT&T Stadium
description: Home of the Dallas Cowboys, known as 'Jerry World'. Features the world's largest retractable roof and one of the biggest high-definition video screens in sports.

name: Levi's Stadium
description: Home of the San Francisco 49ers, featuring green design elements and solar panels. Located in Silicon Valley, it hosted Super Bowl 50.

name: Lumen Field
description: Known for its passionate 12th Man fan culture and loud atmosphere, Lumen Field sits on Elliott Bay with views of the Olympic Mountains.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
description: Home of the Miami Dolphins, completely renovated in 2016. Located in the vibrant South Florida area, it hosted the 2020 Super Bowl.

name: NRG Stadium
description: The first NFL stadium with a retractable roof and natural grass. Home of the Houston Texans and host of multiple Super Bowls.

name: Arrowhead Stadium
description: Home of the Kansas City Chiefs and consistently ranked as one of the loudest stadiums in sports. Located in the heart of America.

name: Lincoln Financial Field
description: Home of the Philadelphia Eagles in the historic city of brotherly love. Located adjacent to Citizens Bank Park and Wells Fargo Center.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
description: Award-winning stadium with a retractable roof that opens like an oculus. Home to the Atlanta Falcons and Atlanta United FC.

name: Gillette Stadium
description: Home of the New England Patriots dynasty and New England Revolution. Located between Boston and Providence.

name: BMO Field
description: Canada's premier football stadium and home of Toronto FC in MLS. Expanded and renovated to meet FIFA World Cup standards.

name: BC Place
description: Western Canada's largest covered stadium with a retractable air-supported roof. Located in the heart of downtown Vancouver.

name: Estadio Akron
description: Home of Club Deportivo Guadalajara (Chivas), one of Mexico's most beloved clubs. A modern stadium in Mexico's second largest city.

name: Estadio BBVA
description: Home of CF Monterrey, built at the foot of the Cerro de la Silla mountain. One of the most scenic and modern stadiums in Latin America.
